title: ranunculus acris subsp. glabriusculus
intro: Ranunculus acris subsp. glabriusculus, also known as the tall buttercup, is a plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and tall, slender stem. The medicinal properties of this plant include its use as a diuretic and its ability to treat fever, inflammation, and skin conditions, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The health benefits associated with Ranunculus acris subsp. glabriusculus include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and even exhibit antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the flowers and leaves in water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
